failure_modes.text: Scale can magnify defects or coordination failures faster than the system can repair them.
failure_modes.source_quote: scale magnifies defects, coordination failures, or mediocre quality
failure_modes.mitigation: Monitor defect rates and coordination costs as volume grows: scale amplifies problems as fast as it amplifies output, so build quality feedback loops that grow with the operation.

failure_modes.text: Teams can confuse busyness with leverage and miss the few activities that actually scale results.
failure_modes.mitigation: Focus scaling effort on the few activities with genuine increasing returns: audit which operations actually become cheaper or better at scale versus those that just get busier.

failure_modes.text: Pursuing more volume can erode the quality or differentiation that made the offering valuable.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect the quality differentiator: set explicit quality floors and brand standards that cannot be traded away in pursuit of volume growth.

premortem_questions.text: Which defect would scale faster than our ability to detect and correct it?
What fixed infrastructure or knowledge cost are we really spreading, and is the volume enough to matter?
premortem_questions.source_quote: spread infrastructure, knowledge, or distribution costs

premortem_questions.text: Are we scaling an advantage, or merely scaling activity?

heuristics.text: Focus on the few revenue, cost, and utilization levers that move the whole system.
Treat the organization like a machine with key measurement points and adjustable levers.
Do not boil the ocean; gather only enough facts to support the needed scaling choice.
